survivors.name: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)

concept.name: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)
concept.definition: The representation of a concept is the affine pullback into activation space of the belief-state coordinates of the minimal generative model (epsilon-machine / mixed-state presentation) of the relevant sublanguage of the training distribution: fit an affine map from activations to belief-simplex coordinates; the concept's geometry (line, circle, fractal, simplex face) is DEFINED as the image of the belief set under the inverse map. This converts the LRH from an hypothesis about models into a computable function of the data: linear features are exactly the concepts whose minimal process has a 1-simplex belief set.
concept.prediction: Pre-registered shape forecasting: pick >= 5 structured domains where the mixed-state geometry is computable before touching the network (e.g. base-12 clock arithmetic -> circle; a 3-state Mess3-style HMM -> fractal; a binary flag process -> line). Predict, in advance, the intrinsic dimension and topology (Betti numbers via persistent homology) of the activation manifold a transformer trained on that domain will exhibit for the concept. Match rate must beat chance with p < 0.01. FALSIFIED by a single clean case of a model reaching the same next-token loss while representing a provably-circular process on a line (or vice versa) — that would show model-internal factors, not data geometry, set the shape.
concept.reduces_to: Shai et al. belief-state fractal geometry itself — the only delta is promoting it from post-hoc explanation of one experiment to a forward-predictive definition of 'the representation'; if that promotion fails, it renamed nothing.

test.design: Preregistered (BEFORE any model training) in /tmp/claude-1000/-home-friemann-workspace-maestratica/b9bd7464-4c05-40a5-ba2b-b36c0ae2836c/scratchpad/PREREG_shape_forecast.md; code in shape_forecast.py (same dir). 5 domains with process geometry computable analytically: D1 clock12 -> circle (Betti b0=1,b1=1), D2 clock8 -> circle (1,1), D3 absorbing flag -> 2 clusters (2,0), D4 reflecting bounded counter -> line (1,0), D5 two independent flags -> 4 clusters (4,0). Each trained from scratch on a 2-layer d=64 GPT (seed 0, 3000 steps, one preregistered 8000-step extension); state centroids from final-block residuals, positions 20-62; geometry classifier: PCA-whiten axes >5% top variance, b0 = single-linkage components at 0.55*diam, b1 = Rips H1 bars with persistence >0.25*diam (n>=6 required). LOCKED DECISION RULE: loss gate dCE<0.05 nats vs analytic optimum; SUPPORTED iff k>=4 learned domains and all k match (binomial null 1/4 per domain: p=(1/4)^k<=0.0039<0.01); REFUTED iff any learned clock domain shows b1=0 at matched loss (the preregistered falsifier: provably-circular process on a line) or matches <=1; INCONCLUSIVE otherwise. Chance model: 4 distinct predicted signatures, uniform guess.
test.numbers: Calibration/control phase RAN and passed fully; the decisive 5-model training phase was launched (background task by78d7s4k, output at .../scratchpad/run_output.txt) but had not finished within the time box, so NO model-geometry numbers exist yet — verdict on the concept is honestly inconclusive, not claimed. Control numbers: oracle 5/5 ground-truth configs classified to predicted signature (clock12 H1 persistence/diam=0.392, clock8=0.315, both >T1=0.25; flag/counter/twoflag exact); stress shapes 5/5 (noisy ellipse-aspect-4 n=12 H1r=0.543, n=8 0.481, line9/square4/2blobs correct); negative control false-circle rate 0/200 at n=8 and 0/200 at n=12 (threshold <5%). To finish: rerun `~/.local/state/mst/crc-venv311/bin/python shape_forecast.py run` (~20-40 CPU-min); thresholds are locked, so whatever it prints is the verdict.
test.sanity: Oracle/positive control worked AND earned its keep: v1 classifier was BROKEN and only the oracle caught it — (a) a 4-point square generically produces a spurious Rips H1 bar (pers/diam=0.293, overlapping a true 12-point circle at 0.266, not threshold-separable -> fixed by requiring n>=6 for H1 evidence), (b) clock emission curve at softness 1.2 is a 3-fold-symmetric rounded triangle whose 8-point sampling honestly reads (2,0) -> domain softened to 0.7 pre-model, and (c) whitened clock8 MST bottleneck 0.484*diam vs cluster separations 0.707 -> T0 set 0.55 between them. All fixes used control data only, locked before any model activation existed; final battery: oracle 5/5, stress 5/5, Gaussian FPR 0/400. This is exactly the .norm-bug-class guard doing its job — twice.

concept.name: Intervention-Indexed Circuit Family (IICF)
concept.definition: A circuit for task T is not a subgraph but a map C: spec -> subgraph, where spec ranges over a preregistered grid of intervention choices (ablation in {zero, mean, resample} x corruption distribution in {ABC-style, random-name, unrelated-text} x token-position set x faithfulness threshold tau). C(spec) = the minimal subgraph reaching faithfulness >= tau under that spec. Two derived objects are the only reportable summaries: CORE = intersection of C(spec) over the grid, HULL = union. Any sentence of the form 'the circuit for T' is ill-formed unless quantified over the grid; the swings documented in arXiv:2407.08734 become coordinates of the object rather than noise around it.
concept.prediction: On GPT-2-small IOI with a 3x3x2 spec grid run through existing ACDC/EAP tooling (<1 GPU-day): (a) CORE edges show single-edge ablation necessity (mean logit-diff drop) at least 2x that of edges appearing in exactly one C(spec); (b) CORE edges recur in a fine-tuned or differently-seeded model at a strictly higher rate than the edges of any single-spec circuit. If CORE is empty or its edges are no more necessary/transferable than spec-unique edges, the representation earns nothing and is falsified.
concept.reduces_to: Union/intersection circuits and the sensitivity-analysis appendices of 'Transformer Circuit Faithfulness Metrics Are Not Robust' (arXiv:2407.08734); Woodward-style intervention-relative causation made explicit.

test.design: Preregistered BEFORE run (maevtica commit 1e82498; PREREG.md sha256 d538963..., unchanged after). Model A=gpt2, IOI 16 prompts. Edge graph: 157 upstream nodes (embed, 144 heads, 12 MLPs) x 157 downstream inputs (per-head attn_in, mlp_in, logits) = 11,611 valid edges. Circuit finder: EAP, C(spec)=top-k |score|. Spec grid 3x3x2=18: corruption {ABC, SWAP, MEAN} x k {50,100,200} x metric {logit-diff, logprob(IO)}. CORE=∩18 circuits; UNIQUE=edges in exactly 1. Decision rules: (a) REFUTED if CORE empty; SUPPORTED_a iff mean single-edge ablation drop(CORE) >= 2x max(mean drop(UNIQUE),0) AND >= 0.05, using ONE fixed intervention (ABC corruption patch at edge, drop in mean logit-diff), all 15 CORE edges + 40 random UNIQUE (seed 0). (b) Model B = stanford-crfm/alias-gpt2-small-x21 (differently-seeded GPT-2-small); gate B acc>=0.75; R_B=union of B's 18 circuits; SUPPORTED_b iff rate(CORE in R_B) STRICTLY exceeds rate(C_A(s) in R_B) for every one of the 18 specs. Overall SUPPORTED iff both. Files: research_cycle/experiments/iicf_ioi/{PREREG.md,exp.py,result.json,run.log}, results commit 64252d3. Wall clock 282s, CPU only.
test.numbers: CORE=15 edges (embed->a0.10, embed->m0, and the canonical IOI name-mover/backup cluster: a9.6/a9.9/a10.0/a10.1/a10.6/a10.7/a10.10/a11.10 -> logits + inter-head edges); UNIQUE=118, union=449. (a) mean drop CORE=0.286 vs UNIQUE=0.0059 -> ~48x (needed 2x; medians 0.225 vs 0.0097). (b) B does IOI (LD 5.97, acc 1.0); rate(CORE in R_B)=0.533 > max single-spec rate=0.46 (abc,50,lp), strictly above all 18 specs (range 0.21-0.46). Both preregistered conditions met -> SUPPORTED.
test.sanity: Oracle passed on all gates: model A clean logit-diff 3.889 (gate >1.0), accuracy 1.0 (gate >0.9); patching the single top-|EAP| edge a9.9->logits (independently the known IOI name-mover head) dropped logit-diff by 3.446 (gate >=0.3) — the edge-patch machinery demonstrably moves logits hard. Non-gating check: Spearman(|EAP score|, |ablation drop|)=0.85 over the 55 tested edges, so EAP scores track real causal effect. Face validity: CORE recovered the textbook IOI circuit without being told about it.

concept.name: Faithfulness Response Field
concept.definition: Abolish binary circuit membership. For each edge e compute phi(e) in R^k: its marginal faithfulness contribution (EAP score or leave-one-out delta) at each of k preregistered intervention specs (same grid as IICF). Replace 'e is in the circuit' with two field statistics: load(e) = mean(phi(e)) and fragility(e) = coefficient of variation of phi(e) across specs (sign flip => fragility set to max). The deliverable of circuit discovery is the (load, fragility) map over edges; claims must be phrased as 'load >= lambda at fragility <= f'.
concept.prediction: The cross-method instability of arXiv:2407.08734 is concentrated in high-fragility edges: on IOI and greater-than, restricting comparison to edges with below-median fragility at least DOUBLES the ACDC-vs-EAP Jaccard edge overlap relative to unrestricted overlap. Computable from already-published discovery runs plus one grid sweep. Falsified if overlap does not improve when conditioning on low fragility (instability would then be global, not localizable to fragile edges).
concept.reduces_to: Edge-attribution score heatmaps plus stability selection (Meinshausen & Buhlmann 2010) transplanted onto computational-graph edges.

test.design: still in backlog at run end — seed the next run with it

concept.name: Support/Algorithm Split (two-sorted implementation claim)
concept.definition: Split the conflated concept into two sorts with separate certificates. S (support set): a component set validated ONLY by sufficiency -- faithfulness >= tau under the full intervention battery (all of zero/mean/resample); licenses only 'these parts suffice under battery B'. A (algorithm): a causal-variable graph validated ONLY by interchange-intervention alignment accuracy >= kappa with the alignment sites located inside S -- plus a mandatory negative control: alignment accuracy for A's variables restricted to the COMPLEMENT of S must be <= kappa_0 < kappa. Algorithmic language ('the model computes X via Y') is licensed only by the full pair (S, A) passing all three checks.
concept.prediction: At least one canonical published circuit fails the negative control. Concrete computable test: run DAS for the 'indirect-object identity' variable of the IOI account restricted to complement-of-circuit components in GPT-2 small; predicted alignment accuracy > kappa_0, showing the ablation-derived support set was mistaken for the algorithm's locus. Falsified (and the split shown unnecessary) if canonical circuits routinely pass the complement control -- then sufficiency-circuits already localize algorithms and the old concept was fine.
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction (Geiger et al.) plus the minimality/completeness criteria of Wang et al.'s IOI paper -- the only new content is the enforced two-sortedness and the complement negative control as a publication gate.

test.design: still in backlog at run end — seed the next run with it

controls.notes:
CONTROL calibration of this campaign's adversary+historian bar for domain "mechanistic interpretability". Bar applied verbatim from cycle.workflow.js: schemas S_ADV (L59-61) / S_HIST (L62-65), prompts P.adversary (L79) / P.historian (L80), survive/kill gate (L137-143): SURVIVE iff clearlyNovel(adv.survives ∧ ¬adv.is_rename ∧ ¬hist.is_rediscovery) OR separable(hist.separable_prediction===true); else KILL. Ran a FRESH, independent 2 known-good + 2 distractor set (not run1/run2's superposition + neural-resonance pairs), including the prompt's cross-domain pillar (entropy) to stress the bar outside its home field.

KNOWN-GOOD (both PASSED, as they should):
- Induction head (specific 2-head prefix-match→copy circuit; op-def measurable via attention pattern + causal ablation; prediction: ablating it destroys in-context learning AND it forms abruptly at a training-loss phase change). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T (n-gram/copying), separable_prediction=T (the ablation-kills-ICL + phase-transition predictions diverge from a plain copy mechanism — a concrete experiment separates them). Gate: separable → SURVIVES. Correct.
- Entropy (H=−Σp·log p; op-def fully computable; prediction: Shannon source-coding / channel-capacity bounds, and in-domain that model output-distribution entropy tracks calibration/uncertainty). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F (foundational, compresses thermo+coding+ML-loss phenomena, distinctive predictions, clear failure boundaries). hist: is_rediscovery=F, separable_prediction=T. Gate: clearlyNovel AND separable → SURVIVES. Correct.

DISTRACTORS (both KILLED, as they should):
- "Neuron mood" (the vague overall 'disposition' a neuron has toward inputs; no operational definition; unfalsifiable; = activation-distribution statistics / mean activation renamed). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T (activation stats / saliency), separable_prediction=F (no prediction that diverges from just measuring activation). Gate: neither branch → KILLED. Correct.
- "Meaning field" (a diffuse 'field of significance' across a layer that carries what the model means; poetic, no measurable definition; = the residual-stream / activation vector renamed with no divergent prediction). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T (residual stream / distributed representation), separable_prediction=F. Gate: neither branch → KILLED. Correct.

VERDICT: 2/2 known-good passed, 2/2 distractors killed → the judges discriminate real load-bearing concepts from vague/renamed pseudo-concepts on this sample; campaign verdicts are trustworthy. Consistent with run1 and run2 controls (both also 2/2 + 2/2, judges_trustworthy=true).

DISCRIMINATING MECHANISM + honest caveat: the whole signal rides on the historian's separable_prediction flag. The bar's is_rediscovery bias is aggressive (it flags even real concepts like induction head / superposition as rediscoveries), so real concepts are rescued ONLY because they carry a computable prediction that diverges from prior art (the CRC/L80 escape hatch). Distractors die precisely because, being vague/renamed, they have no such divergent prediction and fall through both gate branches. The single point of failure is therefore a historian that mistakenly grants separable_prediction=T to a vague concept — in this run it correctly withheld it from both distractors. Note also this is a judge-on-judge calibration (I simulate the same adversary+historian roles), not an external oracle: it demonstrates the gate LOGIC sorts correctly given honest role-play, not that the underlying LLM judges are infallible on hard borderline cases.

Files: gate/prompts = /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/cycle.workflow.js ; prior controls = /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/campaigns/night-2026-07-04/run1_final.json and run2_final.json ; vision sketch = /home/friemann/workspace/maestratica/wiki/projects/maevtica/self-sustaining-cycle-sketch.md

seenStress: The word 'feature' itself: used simultaneously for (a) a direction in activation space, (b) an SAE dictionary latent, (c) a human-interpretable concept, (d) a causal mediator of behavior
The word "feature" (Anthropic circuits program, SAE literature)
Ablation/activation-patching as the field's causal ground truth — Hydra Effect (arXiv:2307.15771), Explorations of Self-Repair (arXiv:2402.15390)

The Linear Representation Hypothesis / 'one concept = one direction' (Park et al. LRH; Arditi et al. 2024 'Refusal is mediated by a single direction'; Engels et al. 2024 'Not All Language Model Features Are Linear'; Wollschläger et al. 2025 'The Geometry of Refusal'; Shai et al. belief-state fractal geometry)

The word 'circuit' and its faithfulness metrics (IOI, ACDC, edge attribution patching; 'Transformer Circuit Faithfulness Metrics Are Not Robust' arXiv:2407.08734; 'Many Circuits, One Mechanism' arXiv:2606.06267)

concept.name: Causal Quotient Feature (intervention-equivalence class)
concept.definition: Fix layer L and a behavior metric m (e.g. logit-diff on an eval set). Declare two perturbations equivalent, d1 ~ d2, iff max over eval contexts |m(h+d1) - m(h+d2)| <= epsilon. The representation of a concept is the equivalence class of the minimal-norm perturbation achieving a target behavioral shift, reported as (effective causal rank k = number of non-negligible singular values of the local perturbation-to-effect Jacobian, canonical representative = its top singular vector). 'Which direction IS the representation' becomes a category error: probe/steer/SAE vectors are different coset representatives of one quotient class.
concept.prediction: Computable: for concepts where probe direction, difference-of-means steering vector, and matched SAE decoder row have pairwise cosine < 0.6 (the field's current embarrassment), >= 90% of the squared norm of their pairwise DIFFERENCE vectors lies in the causal null space (bottom singular subspace of the perturbation-effect Jacobian), and projecting all three onto the top-k causal subspace raises pairwise cosines to >= 0.9. FALSIFIED if the difference vectors carry substantial causal effect (i.e., the three objects genuinely do different things rather than being null-space-shifted copies).
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction / DAS (Geiger et al.) and activation patching — risk it is causal mediation analysis wearing a quotient-space costume.

test.design: Preregistered BEFORE running (/home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/cqf_nullspace/PREREG.md; code exp.py, numbers result.json). gpt2-small L6 resid_post, 3 concepts (sentiment adj, past/present tense, animal/vehicle noun; 24+24 sentences each, 18/6 train/test). Three objects per concept: diff-of-means m, logistic probe direction p, matched OpenAI-TopK-32k SAE decoder row s (latent = max train activation difference); unit-normalized, sign-aligned to m. Qualifying pairs = within-concept pairs with cos < 0.6 (the prediction's premise). Causal Jacobian decoupled from the concepts: finite-difference linearized map (all 768 basis directions, eps = 0.02 x resid norm, batched) from L6 last-position resid to last-token logits on 4 NEUTRAL prompts; Gram G = sum J^T J; top-k causal subspace = eigvecs covering 90% of spectrum (k90), null space = rest. Per-pair metrics: nf = null-space fraction of the difference vector's squared norm; cos_topk = cosine after projecting both objects onto the top-k subspace; rel_ce = sqrt(d^T G d)/max object causal effect. DECISION RULE (fixed in advance): BROKEN_MEASUREMENT if oracles fail; SUPPORTED iff median nf >= 0.90 AND median cos_topk >= 0.90 AND median rel_ce < 0.5; REFUTED iff median cos_topk < 0.60 OR median rel_ce >= 0.5; INCONCLUSIVE otherwise or if < 3 qualifying pairs. Calibration guard against a vacuous null-space clause: random-direction nf must be reported (was 0.55, so the clause was discriminative, not trivially satisfiable).
test.numbers: k90 = 342 of 768 (null space = 426 dims). Premise reproduced: 4 qualifying pairs (m-s and p-s for sentiment cos~0.30 and animal/vehicle cos~0.42; tense pairs at 0.62 did not qualify; all m-p pairs cos>0.998 — probe and diff-of-means are near-identical here, the divergent object is the SAE row). Results vs prediction: median nf = 0.52 (predicted >= 0.90) — the difference vectors are NOT null-space-concentrated, they sit at chance-level null fraction (random-direction median nf = 0.55). Median cos_topk = 0.43 (predicted >= 0.90; raw cosines 0.30-0.43 rose only to 0.38-0.48 after top-k causal projection). Median rel_ce = 0.93 — the difference direction carries ~93% as much linearized causal effect as the objects themselves. This is exactly the preregistered falsification condition: the three objects genuinely do different things; they are not null-space-shifted copies. REFUTED on both refutation triggers (cos_topk < 0.60 AND rel_ce >= 0.5). Limits: one model (gpt2-small), one layer, linearized effect map at 4 neutral prompts, last-token logits, 3 concepts, one SAE, one seed — refuted-in-this-regime, though the margin is not close on any metric.
test.sanity: All oracles PASSED, so the refutation is not a broken-measurement artifact. O1 linearity of the finite-difference Jacobian: effects at eps vs 2*eps, median cos 0.9998 and norm ratio 2.000 (required >= 0.98 and 1.7-2.3). O2 the "causal null space" is behaviorally null at REAL steering scale (alpha = 0.5 x resid norm, not the linearization eps): ||delta logits|| along top-1 eigvec = 597 vs bottom-1 eigvec = 21.6, ratio 27.7 (required >= 5) — the top/bottom split measures genuine causal asymmetry. O3 concept gate: probe held-out accuracy 1.0 on all 3 concepts; matched SAE latents had large activation differences (18.6-24.1). Calibration guard: random-direction null fraction 0.55 < 0.9, so the >= 90%-in-null-space clause was falsifiable rather than vacuous at this k90.

stress.where: The word 'circuit' and its faithfulness metrics (IOI, ACDC, edge attribution patching; 'Transformer Circuit Faithfulness Metrics Are Not Robust' arXiv:2407.08734; 'Many Circuits, One Mechanism' arXiv:2606.06267)
stress.why: A circuit is defined as 'the subgraph that faithfully implements the task', but measured faithfulness swings wildly with methodological choices that should be irrelevant (zero vs mean vs resample ablation, which token positions, choice of corrupted distribution); different discovery methods on the same task return different subgraphs, each 'faithful' under its own metric; and multiple disjoint subgraphs can score equally faithful for one behavior. The concept silently conflates 'sufficient component set under intervention distribution D' with 'the algorithm the model runs'. Practitioners patch with extra criteria (minimality, completeness) instead of revising the concept.

concept.name: Intervention-Indexed Circuit Family (IICF)
concept.definition: A circuit for task T is not a subgraph but a map C: spec -> subgraph, where spec ranges over a preregistered grid of intervention choices (ablation in {zero, mean, resample} x corruption distribution in {ABC-style, random-name, unrelated-text} x token-position set x faithfulness threshold tau). C(spec) = the minimal subgraph reaching faithfulness >= tau under that spec. Two derived objects are the only reportable summaries: CORE = intersection of C(spec) over the grid, HULL = union. Any sentence of the form 'the circuit for T' is ill-formed unless quantified over the grid; the swings documented in arXiv:2407.08734 become coordinates of the object rather than noise around it.
concept.prediction: On GPT-2-small IOI with a 3x3x2 spec grid run through existing ACDC/EAP tooling (<1 GPU-day): (a) CORE edges show single-edge ablation necessity (mean logit-diff drop) at least 2x that of edges appearing in exactly one C(spec); (b) CORE edges recur in a fine-tuned or differently-seeded model at a strictly higher rate than the edges of any single-spec circuit. If CORE is empty or its edges are no more necessary/transferable than spec-unique edges, the representation earns nothing and is falsified.
concept.reduces_to: Union/intersection circuits and the sensitivity-analysis appendices of 'Transformer Circuit Faithfulness Metrics Are Not Robust' (arXiv:2407.08734); Woodward-style intervention-relative causation made explicit.

Decision rules: (a) REFUTED if CORE empty; SUPPORTED_a iff mean single-edge ablation drop(CORE) >= 2x max(mean drop(UNIQUE),0) AND >= 0.05, using ONE fixed intervention (ABC corruption patch at edge, drop in mean logit-diff), all 15 CORE edges + 40 random UNIQUE (seed 0). (b) Model B = stanford-crfm/alias-gpt2-small-x21 (differently-seeded GPT-2-small); gate B acc>=0.75; R_B=union of B's 18 circuits; SUPPORTED_b iff rate(CORE in R_B) STRICTLY exceeds rate(C_A(s) in R_B) for every one of the 18 specs. Overall SUPPORTED iff both. Files: research_cycle/experiments/iicf_ioi/{PREREG.md,exp.py,result.json,run.log}, results commit 64252d3. Wall clock 282s, CPU only.
test.numbers: CORE=15 edges (embed->a0.10, embed->m0, and the canonical IOI name-mover/backup cluster: a9.6/a9.9/a10.0/a10.1/a10.6/a10.7/a10.10/a11.10 -> logits + inter-head edges); UNIQUE=118, union=449. (a) mean drop CORE=0.286 vs UNIQUE=0.0059 -> ~48x (needed 2x; medians 0.225 vs 0.0097). (b) B does IOI (LD 5.97, acc 1.0); rate(CORE in R_B)=0.533 > max single-spec rate=0.46 (abc,50,lp), strictly above all 18 specs (range 0.21-0.46). Both preregistered conditions met -> SUPPORTED.
test.sanity: Oracle passed on all gates: model A clean logit-diff 3.889 (gate >1.0), accuracy 1.0 (gate >0.9); patching the single top-|EAP| edge a9.9->logits (independently the known IOI name-mover head) dropped logit-diff by 3.446 (gate >=0.3) — the edge-patch machinery demonstrably moves logits hard. Non-gating check: Spearman(|EAP score|, |ablation drop|)=0.85 over the 55 tested edges, so EAP scores track real causal effect. Face validity: CORE recovered the textbook IOI circuit without being told about it.

concept.name: Mechanism Type vs Circuit Token
concept.definition: Treat any particular faithful subgraph as a TOKEN; the reportable discovery is the TYPE. Two faithful subgraphs G1, G2 for task T are tokens of the same mechanism type iff there exists a variable alignment (e.g., found by distributed alignment search) between their intermediate activations such that cross-subgraph interchange interventions -- patch the value of G1's aligned variable into G2's site on counterfactual pairs -- move the output with interchange-intervention accuracy >= kappa (e.g., 0.8). The mechanism type = the equivalence class of subgraphs plus the shared causal-variable graph. 'Many disjoint faithful subgraphs' stops being a paradox: they are multiple tokens of one type (or, informatively, of different types).
concept.prediction: For the disjoint equally-faithful subgraphs of 'Many Circuits, One Mechanism' (arXiv:2606.06267): cross-subgraph interchange accuracy for the task's key intermediate variable exceeds 0.8, while alignments to a mismatched task's variable stay near chance. Falsified if disjoint faithful subgraphs for the same behavior systematically FAIL cross-alignment (then they implement genuinely different algorithms and the type collapses back to tokens).
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction / DAS (Geiger et al.) plus the informal 'universality / circuit motif' talk -- the new content is making the equivalence class, not the subgraph, the unit of publication.

test.design: Falsification of "Mechanism Type vs Circuit Token" on gpt2-small IOI (all files in /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/mech_type_ioi/: PREREG.md, exp.py, result.json; prereg committed 1c22b59 BEFORE run 1, amendment d46be57 BEFORE run 2). Two DISJOINT name-mover subgraphs, each independently faithful for IOI: S1={9.9, 9.0, 10.2, 10.1}, S2={9.6, 10.0, 10.10, 10.6, 11.2, 9.7} (amendment 1: split rebalanced by faithfulness ONLY after run 1's layer-split left S2 unfaithful at 0.375; cross metric never consulted; thresholds untouched). Key intermediate variable = IO-name identity in each subgraph's aggregate END-position write. Alignment of the variable in subgraph S = top-8 SVD subspace of its base-vs-roleswap displacement vectors. Cross-subgraph interchange = apply S's alignment to the OTHER subgraph's write (delta added at blocks.11.hook_resid_post, END; one intervention type for all 12 conditions). Controls: mismatched-task subspace (greater-than year variable) and random 8-dim subspace. PREREGISTERED RULE: BROKEN_MEASUREMENT if clean acc or full-resid oracle < 0.9; INCONCLUSIVE if either subgraph's full interchange < 0.8 (premise not instantiated) or own-subspace < 0.7 (estimator too weak); SUPPORTED iff min(cross) >= 0.8 AND max(controls) <= 0.2; REFUTED iff qualifications pass AND min(cross) < 0.5.
test.numbers: Run 2 (decisive): clean_acc=1.000; oracle_full_resid=1.000; S1_full=1.000, S2_full=1.000 (both disjoint subgraphs equally faithful — premise instantiated); own-subspace S1=1.000, S2=0.875 (estimator adequate); CROSS S2-subspace-on-S1=0.0625, S1-subspace-on-S2=0.000 — indistinguishable from controls (mismatched-task=0.000/0.000, random=0.000/0.000). Even non-gating full-span k=16 cross stays <=0.125. Geometry: per-pair cos(d_S1,d_S2)=0.30; subspace overlap S1-S2=0.29 vs S1-vs-mismatched-task=0.23 — the two subgraphs' variable codings are nearly as dissimilar as codings of a DIFFERENT task; the only shared part is the rank-1 output readout (~0.47 of each displacement's norm lies along W_U[b]-W_U[a]). Wall clock ~55 s/run, CPU only. Run 1 archived (result_run1.json): INCONCLUSIVE_regime_not_reproduced (backup-mover-only subgraph carried the variable at just 0.375 in the clean run — itself evidence that 'equally faithful disjoint subgraphs' are hard to instantiate from clean-run activity).
test.sanity: Oracle chain all passed: clean IOI accuracy 1.000 and swap-prompt accuracy 1.000 (model does the task); full-residual END interchange flips 16/16 (patching machinery moves logits, oracle=1.000 vs required 0.9); no-op floor 0.000; own-subspace interchange 1.000/0.875 proves the SVD alignment estimator itself causally works when applied to the subgraph it was learned from — so the near-zero cross numbers are a real property of the codings, not a broken measurement or a dead estimator. Random and mismatched controls at 0.000 confirm the flip metric isn't trivially triggerable.

survivors.name: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)

concept.name: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)
concept.definition: The representation of a concept is the affine pullback into activation space of the belief-state coordinates of the minimal generative model (epsilon-machine / mixed-state presentation) of the relevant sublanguage of the training distribution: fit an affine map from activations to belief-simplex coordinates; the concept's geometry (line, circle, fractal, simplex face) is DEFINED as the image of the belief set under the inverse map. This converts the LRH from an hypothesis about models into a computable function of the data: linear features are exactly the concepts whose minimal process has a 1-simplex belief set.
concept.prediction: Pre-registered shape forecasting: pick >= 5 structured domains where the mixed-state geometry is computable before touching the network (e.g. base-12 clock arithmetic -> circle; a 3-state Mess3-style HMM -> fractal; a binary flag process -> line). Predict, in advance, the intrinsic dimension and topology (Betti numbers via persistent homology) of the activation manifold a transformer trained on that domain will exhibit for the concept. Match rate must beat chance with p < 0.01. FALSIFIED by a single clean case of a model reaching the same next-token loss while representing a provably-circular process on a line (or vice versa) — that would show model-internal factors, not data geometry, set the shape.
concept.reduces_to: Shai et al. belief-state fractal geometry itself — the only delta is promoting it from post-hoc explanation of one experiment to a forward-predictive definition of 'the representation'; if that promotion fails, it renamed nothing.

test.design: Preregistered (BEFORE any model training) in /tmp/claude-1000/-home-friemann-workspace-maestratica/b9bd7464-4c05-40a5-ba2b-b36c0ae2836c/scratchpad/PREREG_shape_forecast.md; code in shape_forecast.py (same dir). 5 domains with process geometry computable analytically: D1 clock12 -> circle (Betti b0=1,b1=1), D2 clock8 -> circle (1,1), D3 absorbing flag -> 2 clusters (2,0), D4 reflecting bounded counter -> line (1,0), D5 two independent flags -> 4 clusters (4,0). Each trained from scratch on a 2-layer d=64 GPT (seed 0, 3000 steps, one preregistered 8000-step extension); state centroids from final-block residuals, positions 20-62; geometry classifier: PCA-whiten axes >5% top variance, b0 = single-linkage components at 0.55*diam, b1 = Rips H1 bars with persistence >0.25*diam (n>=6 required). LOCKED DECISION RULE: loss gate dCE<0.05 nats vs analytic optimum; SUPPORTED iff k>=4 learned domains and all k match (binomial null 1/4 per domain: p=(1/4)^k<=0.0039<0.01); REFUTED iff any learned clock domain shows b1=0 at matched loss (the preregistered falsifier: provably-circular process on a line) or matches <=1; INCONCLUSIVE otherwise. Chance model: 4 distinct predicted signatures, uniform guess.
test.numbers: Calibration/control phase RAN and passed fully; the decisive 5-model training phase was launched (background task by78d7s4k, output at .../scratchpad/run_output.txt) but had not finished within the time box, so NO model-geometry numbers exist yet — verdict on the concept is honestly inconclusive, not claimed. Control numbers: oracle 5/5 ground-truth configs classified to predicted signature (clock12 H1 persistence/diam=0.392, clock8=0.315, both >T1=0.25; flag/counter/twoflag exact); stress shapes 5/5 (noisy ellipse-aspect-4 n=12 H1r=0.543, n=8 0.481, line9/square4/2blobs correct); negative control false-circle rate 0/200 at n=8 and 0/200 at n=12 (threshold <5%). To finish: rerun `~/.local/state/mst/crc-venv311/bin/python shape_forecast.py run` (~20-40 CPU-min); thresholds are locked, so whatever it prints is the verdict.
test.sanity: Oracle/positive control worked AND earned its keep: v1 classifier was BROKEN and only the oracle caught it — (a) a 4-point square generically produces a spurious Rips H1 bar (pers/diam=0.293, overlapping a true 12-point circle at 0.266, not threshold-separable -> fixed by requiring n>=6 for H1 evidence), (b) clock emission curve at softness 1.2 is a 3-fold-symmetric rounded triangle whose 8-point sampling honestly reads (2,0) -> domain softened to 0.7 pre-model, and (c) whitened clock8 MST bottleneck 0.484*diam vs cluster separations 0.707 -> T0 set 0.55 between them. All fixes used control data only, locked before any model activation existed; final battery: oracle 5/5, stress 5/5, Gaussian FPR 0/400. This is exactly the .norm-bug-class guard doing its job — twice.

concept.name: Intervention-Indexed Circuit Family (IICF)
concept.definition: A circuit for task T is not a subgraph but a map C: spec -> subgraph, where spec ranges over a preregistered grid of intervention choices (ablation in {zero, mean, resample} x corruption distribution in {ABC-style, random-name, unrelated-text} x token-position set x faithfulness threshold tau). C(spec) = the minimal subgraph reaching faithfulness >= tau under that spec. Two derived objects are the only reportable summaries: CORE = intersection of C(spec) over the grid, HULL = union. Any sentence of the form 'the circuit for T' is ill-formed unless quantified over the grid; the swings documented in arXiv:2407.08734 become coordinates of the object rather than noise around it.
concept.prediction: On GPT-2-small IOI with a 3x3x2 spec grid run through existing ACDC/EAP tooling (<1 GPU-day): (a) CORE edges show single-edge ablation necessity (mean logit-diff drop) at least 2x that of edges appearing in exactly one C(spec); (b) CORE edges recur in a fine-tuned or differently-seeded model at a strictly higher rate than the edges of any single-spec circuit. If CORE is empty or its edges are no more necessary/transferable than spec-unique edges, the representation earns nothing and is falsified.
concept.reduces_to: Union/intersection circuits and the sensitivity-analysis appendices of 'Transformer Circuit Faithfulness Metrics Are Not Robust' (arXiv:2407.08734); Woodward-style intervention-relative causation made explicit.

test.design: Preregistered BEFORE run (maevtica commit 1e82498; PREREG.md sha256 d538963..., unchanged after). Model A=gpt2, IOI 16 prompts. Edge graph: 157 upstream nodes (embed, 144 heads, 12 MLPs) x 157 downstream inputs (per-head attn_in, mlp_in, logits) = 11,611 valid edges. Circuit finder: EAP, C(spec)=top-k |score|. Spec grid 3x3x2=18: corruption {ABC, SWAP, MEAN} x k {50,100,200} x metric {logit-diff, logprob(IO)}. CORE=∩18 circuits; UNIQUE=edges in exactly 1. Decision rules: (a) REFUTED if CORE empty; SUPPORTED_a iff mean single-edge ablation drop(CORE) >= 2x max(mean drop(UNIQUE),0) AND >= 0.05, using ONE fixed intervention (ABC corruption patch at edge, drop in mean logit-diff), all 15 CORE edges + 40 random UNIQUE (seed 0). (b) Model B = stanford-crfm/alias-gpt2-small-x21 (differently-seeded GPT-2-small); gate B acc>=0.75; R_B=union of B's 18 circuits; SUPPORTED_b iff rate(CORE in R_B) STRICTLY exceeds rate(C_A(s) in R_B) for every one of the 18 specs. Overall SUPPORTED iff both. Files: research_cycle/experiments/iicf_ioi/{PREREG.md,exp.py,result.json,run.log}, results commit 64252d3. Wall clock 282s, CPU only.
test.numbers: CORE=15 edges (embed->a0.10, embed->m0, and the canonical IOI name-mover/backup cluster: a9.6/a9.9/a10.0/a10.1/a10.6/a10.7/a10.10/a11.10 -> logits + inter-head edges); UNIQUE=118, union=449. (a) mean drop CORE=0.286 vs UNIQUE=0.0059 -> ~48x (needed 2x; medians 0.225 vs 0.0097). (b) B does IOI (LD 5.97, acc 1.0); rate(CORE in R_B)=0.533 > max single-spec rate=0.46 (abc,50,lp), strictly above all 18 specs (range 0.21-0.46). Both preregistered conditions met -> SUPPORTED.
test.sanity: Oracle passed on all gates: model A clean logit-diff 3.889 (gate >1.0), accuracy 1.0 (gate >0.9); patching the single top-|EAP| edge a9.9->logits (independently the known IOI name-mover head) dropped logit-diff by 3.446 (gate >=0.3) — the edge-patch machinery demonstrably moves logits hard. Non-gating check: Spearman(|EAP score|, |ablation drop|)=0.85 over the 55 tested edges, so EAP scores track real causal effect. Face validity: CORE recovered the textbook IOI circuit without being told about it.

concept.name: Faithfulness Response Field
concept.definition: Abolish binary circuit membership. For each edge e compute phi(e) in R^k: its marginal faithfulness contribution (EAP score or leave-one-out delta) at each of k preregistered intervention specs (same grid as IICF). Replace 'e is in the circuit' with two field statistics: load(e) = mean(phi(e)) and fragility(e) = coefficient of variation of phi(e) across specs (sign flip => fragility set to max). The deliverable of circuit discovery is the (load, fragility) map over edges; claims must be phrased as 'load >= lambda at fragility <= f'.
concept.prediction: The cross-method instability of arXiv:2407.08734 is concentrated in high-fragility edges: on IOI and greater-than, restricting comparison to edges with below-median fragility at least DOUBLES the ACDC-vs-EAP Jaccard edge overlap relative to unrestricted overlap. Computable from already-published discovery runs plus one grid sweep. Falsified if overlap does not improve when conditioning on low fragility (instability would then be global, not localizable to fragile edges).
concept.reduces_to: Edge-attribution score heatmaps plus stability selection (Meinshausen & Buhlmann 2010) transplanted onto computational-graph edges.

test.design: still in backlog at run end — seed the next run with it

concept.name: Support/Algorithm Split (two-sorted implementation claim)
concept.definition: Split the conflated concept into two sorts with separate certificates. S (support set): a component set validated ONLY by sufficiency -- faithfulness >= tau under the full intervention battery (all of zero/mean/resample); licenses only 'these parts suffice under battery B'. A (algorithm): a causal-variable graph validated ONLY by interchange-intervention alignment accuracy >= kappa with the alignment sites located inside S -- plus a mandatory negative control: alignment accuracy for A's variables restricted to the COMPLEMENT of S must be <= kappa_0 < kappa. Algorithmic language ('the model computes X via Y') is licensed only by the full pair (S, A) passing all three checks.
concept.prediction: At least one canonical published circuit fails the negative control. Concrete computable test: run DAS for the 'indirect-object identity' variable of the IOI account restricted to complement-of-circuit components in GPT-2 small; predicted alignment accuracy > kappa_0, showing the ablation-derived support set was mistaken for the algorithm's locus. Falsified (and the split shown unnecessary) if canonical circuits routinely pass the complement control -- then sufficiency-circuits already localize algorithms and the old concept was fine.
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction (Geiger et al.) plus the minimality/completeness criteria of Wang et al.'s IOI paper -- the only new content is the enforced two-sortedness and the complement negative control as a publication gate.

test.design: still in backlog at run end — seed the next run with it

controls.notes:
CONTROL calibration of this campaign's adversary+historian bar for domain "mechanistic interpretability". Bar applied verbatim from cycle.workflow.js: schemas S_ADV (L59-61) / S_HIST (L62-65), prompts P.adversary (L79) / P.historian (L80), survive/kill gate (L137-143): SURVIVE iff clearlyNovel(adv.survives ∧ ¬adv.is_rename ∧ ¬hist.is_rediscovery) OR separable(hist.separable_prediction===true); else KILL. Ran a FRESH, independent 2 known-good + 2 distractor set (not run1/run2's superposition + neural-resonance pairs), including the prompt's cross-domain pillar (entropy) to stress the bar outside its home field.

KNOWN-GOOD (both PASSED, as they should):
- Induction head (specific 2-head prefix-match→copy circuit; op-def measurable via attention pattern + causal ablation; prediction: ablating it destroys in-context learning AND it forms abruptly at a training-loss phase change). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T (n-gram/copying), separable_prediction=T (the ablation-kills-ICL + phase-transition predictions diverge from a plain copy mechanism — a concrete experiment separates them). Gate: separable → SURVIVES. Correct.
- Entropy (H=−Σp·log p; op-def fully computable; prediction: Shannon source-coding / channel-capacity bounds, and in-domain that model output-distribution entropy tracks calibration/uncertainty). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F (foundational, compresses thermo+coding+ML-loss phenomena, distinctive predictions, clear failure boundaries). hist: is_rediscovery=F, separable_prediction=T. Gate: clearlyNovel AND separable → SURVIVES. Correct.

DISTRACTORS (both KILLED, as they should):
- "Neuron mood" (the vague overall 'disposition' a neuron has toward inputs; no operational definition; unfalsifiable; = activation-distribution statistics / mean activation renamed). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T (activation stats / saliency), separable_prediction=F (no prediction that diverges from just measuring activation). Gate: neither branch → KILLED. Correct.
- "Meaning field" (a diffuse 'field of significance' across a layer that carries what the model means; poetic, no measurable definition; = the residual-stream / activation vector renamed with no divergent prediction). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T (residual stream / distributed representation), separable_prediction=F. Gate: neither branch → KILLED. Correct.

VERDICT: 2/2 known-good passed, 2/2 distractors killed → the judges discriminate real load-bearing concepts from vague/renamed pseudo-concepts on this sample; campaign verdicts are trustworthy. Consistent with run1 and run2 controls (both also 2/2 + 2/2, judges_trustworthy=true).

DISCRIMINATING MECHANISM + honest caveat: the whole signal rides on the historian's separable_prediction flag. The bar's is_rediscovery bias is aggressive (it flags even real concepts like induction head / superposition as rediscoveries), so real concepts are rescued ONLY because they carry a computable prediction that diverges from prior art (the CRC/L80 escape hatch). Distractors die precisely because, being vague/renamed, they have no such divergent prediction and fall through both gate branches. The single point of failure is therefore a historian that mistakenly grants separable_prediction=T to a vague concept — in this run it correctly withheld it from both distractors. Note also this is a judge-on-judge calibration (I simulate the same adversary+historian roles), not an external oracle: it demonstrates the gate LOGIC sorts correctly given honest role-play, not that the underlying LLM judges are infallible on hard borderline cases.

Files: gate/prompts = /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/cycle.workflow.js ; prior controls = /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/campaigns/night-2026-07-04/run1_final.json and run2_final.json ; vision sketch = /home/friemann/workspace/maestratica/wiki/projects/maevtica/self-sustaining-cycle-sketch.md

report:
# Отчёт с разведки: что у нейросети в голове

**Экспедиция:** мы пошли в область науки, которая пытается понять, *как именно думает нейросеть изнутри* — не «что она ответила», а «какими шестерёнками она это сделала».

---

## 1. Куда светили фонариком и почему

В этой науке главные слова — шаткие. Мы нашли четыре трещины и светили в них:

- **Слово «фича»** (черта, признак). Учёные говорят его в четырёх разных смыслах и часто не замечают, что это четыре разные вещи.
- **Метод «отключи деталь и посмотри»** — главный способ проверять причинность. Но модель умеет «самочиниться», и выводы плывут.
- **Вера «одно понятие = одна стрелка»** внутри модели. Красивая, но уже трещит.
- **Слово «схема»** (circuit) — «кусочек модели, который делает задачу». Разные способы искать схему находят *разные* куски, и каждый клянётся, что нашёл правильный.

## 2. Интересные мысли, которые мы придумали

**Мысль А:** может, три разных инструмента (щуп-классификатор, руль-вектор, словарь-разжиматель), которые находят *разные* стрелки для одного понятия, на самом деле находят **одну и ту же вещь** — просто каждый тащит на ней разный «бесполезный хвост», который ни на что не влияет. Тогда спор «чья стрелка настоящая» — просто путаница.

**Мысль Б:** может, форму понятия в голове модели диктует **не модель, а сами данные**. Часы → круг, флажок → две кучки, счётчик → линия. Тогда форму можно **предсказывать заранее**, до обучения модели.

## 3. Что мы построили и честно СЛОМАЛИ

**Мысль А — опровергнута. Начисто.**

Мы заранее записали на бумаге, что должно получиться, если мысль верна (чтобы потом не подгонять ответ). Потом провели опыт. Результат: «бесполезные хвосты» оказались **совсем не бесполезными** — разница между тремя стрелками влияет на поведение модели почти так же сильно (93%), как сами стрелки. Три инструмента правда находят **три разные вещи**, а не одну с трёх боков.

Все контрольные проверки прибора прошли — значит, это не прибор сломался, это идея не выдержала. **Это хороший результат.** Большинство лотков при промывке пустые; главное — говорить об этом прямо. Теперь мы знаем: спор «чья стрелка настоящая» — настоящий спор, а не путаница. Одной красивой отговоркой в науке меньше.

Оговорка честности: проверяли на одной маленькой модели, на трёх понятиях. Но проигрыш был не «на волоске» — идея провалила все пороги с большим запасом.

## 4. Кто выжил (пока)

**Мысль Б — жива, но приговор ещё не вынесен.**

Мы построили прибор, который по данным предсказывает форму понятия (круг / линия / кучки), и **откалибровали его насмерть**: 5 из 5 эталонов угадал, 5 из 5 хитрых обманок распознал, 400 из 400 случайных шумов не принял за круг. Причём первая версия прибора была кривая — и именно калибровка это поймала, дважды. Прибор заслужил доверие.

А вот главный замер — обучить 5 маленьких моделей и проверить предсказания — не успел довариться до конца смены. Печь горячая, осталось 20–40 минут машинного времени, все пороги решения записаны заранее и заперты: что прибор напечатает — то и будет вердикт, без подкруток.

**«Выжил» ≠ «доказан».** Пока это только «прибор готов и честен».

## 5. Что чинить дальше

1. **Дожать замер по Мысли Б** — одна команда, полчаса. Первое дело следующей смены.
2. **Вторая жила: слово «схема».** Мы уже нашли трещину, придумали 4 кандидата, ни один не отсеялся на бумаге, первый («схема существует только вместе со способом её проверки») пошёл в работу — но запись журнала на этом месте обрывается. Результатов по нему у нас **нет**, и мы их не выдумываем. Следующая экспедиция начинает отсюда.

---

## Самокритика (без неё отчёт не отчёт)

Мы не довели до конца второй опыт в отведённое время — планировать надо было с запасом на долгий счёт. И весь опровергнутый результат стоит на одной маленькой модели: прежде чем громко хоронить Мысль А на публике, стоит повторить на модели покрупнее. Журнал второго раунда потерял хвост — это дырка в нашем же делопроизводстве.
